name: Incredible Edible (Mon) G11 Coach-Mentor
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger (MS) students. Interested students will be interviewed. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to sustainable development through previous action or service learning. The selected students will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Grade 9-10 Incredible Edible groups. The core activities are very practical - propagating edible plants in the nursery, sowing seeds and planting cuttings. You will also remove weeds from planting spaces, prepare the soil, plant out seedlings, and do mulching and watering as necessary. The Grade 11 coach-mentors must be a "hands on" role model but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objective of a sustainable future. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings.

name: UWCSEA Rainforest Restoration Project (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for a Grade 11 student who will coach G9/10 students doing the service. The UWCSEA Rainforest Restoration Project (URRP) collaborates with local partners to help boost Singapore's tree cover and spread awareness of the importance of trees for the ecosystem, society, economy and personal wellbeing. Interested students will be interviewed by URRP teaching staff. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to environmental conservation and some practical skills in plant cultivation (preferably with trees). The selected student will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Rainforest Restoration group. This will include hands on tasks such as raising and caring for tree saplings in our College nursery, planting and mulching trees, monitoring tree growth, and removing invasive creepers from young trees. However, as a Grade 11 coach-mentor you will also be expected to learn composting skills and train the URRP group, as well as do additional research into Southeast Asian trees and share this with the group.

name: MINDS Faber (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: "This is a selective leadership position for a Grade 11 student who will coach and mentor Middle School students doing the service. Interested students will be interviewed by Mrs Sally Newman (MS Service Coordinator). To be considered, you will ideally have experience of doing service with people with intellectual disabilities. You will need to show evidence of the skills and qualities to mentor others. The Grade 11 coach-mentor must be both a role model for the younger students and someone who observes them and provides positive feedback. The coach-mentor must also accept guidance from the teacher leading the service.The group will be responsible for building a relationship of trust with their MINDS friends. They will also need to take responsibility for planning appropriate art/craft activities from week to week. We have lots of materials available. The Grade 11 coach-mentor should coordinate a reflection and planning session at the end of each week s action . In addition, they should lead a more formal SWOT reflection around November and March, ideally including representatives from MINDS.In summary, this is a role for a committed, mature and sensitive individual who will aim to make the weekly interactions as positive as possible for our MINDS friends.

name: MINDS Ang Mo Kio (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective leadership position for a Grade 11 student who will coach and mentor Middle School students doing the service. You will need to show evidence of the skills and qualities to mentor others. The Grade 11 coach-mentor must be both a role model for the younger students and someone who observes them and provides positive feedback. The coach-mentor must also accept guidance from the teacher/s leading the service.The group will be responsible for building a relationship of trust with their MINDS friends. They will also need to take responsibility for planning appropriate art/craft activities from week to week. We have lots of materials available. The Grade 11 coach-mentor should coordinate a reflection and planning session at the end of each week s action . In addition, they should lead a more formal SWOT reflection around November and March, ideally including representatives from MINDS Ang Mo Kio.In summary, this is a role for a committed, mature and sensitive individual who will aim to make the weekly interactions as positive as possible for our MINDS friends.

name: Genesis in Junior School (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: NOTE:You can only do this service if you are free during Thurs. lunchtime to help set up play spaces and equipment. If you have a GC/activity, this service is not for you.Children from Genesis Special School (for children with multiple disabilities) will visit our primary school every week to play and learn with one Grade 4 and one Grade 5 class. The purpose of the visit is to provide a natural interaction between the two sets of children, helping to integrate the Genesis children with non-disabled peers and expanding UWCSEA children s empathy and understanding of diversity.The Grade 11 coach-mentors will be responsible for mentoring the JS children, but also for role modelling positive interaction with the visiting children. They will be expected to plan and lead suitable activities with some guidance from JS teachers. They should also lead an occasional reflection session after each session, following a format such as What/ So what? Now what? .Although there will be a need for 3-4 Grade 11 coach-mentors in each of the two classes, it is important to emphasise that this is not a suitable service for friends who want to socialise under the guise of doing service. Your responsibility is to serve both the visiting children and the JS children well; if you fall short of that, you will need to move to a different service.

name: Riding for the Disabled Association (RDA)
description: RDA provides a very structured riding therapy programme for children and young people with a wide range of disabilities, led by specialised staff, supported by volunteers. UWCSEA students have two different volunteer roles - one is to work with the horses as a leader, and the other is as a side walker who works with the riders. Each is a role of significant responsibility, and equally vital to the success of the rider's experience. Volunteers find this a very rewarding and fulfilling service to be involved in as they frequently see progress in their rider over the weeks, particularly when there it the chance of sustained partnering with both horse, leader and side walkers which results in the development of trust and rapport between everyone involved. The same children attend weekly for a period of time. No experience with horses is required as substantial training is provided over a few weeks. Real willingness to learn, to connect with others and to be prepared to stretch beyond your comfort zone are more important criteria. We also have opportunities for fund raising for RDA at college events. Sessions end at 5.30pm with a bus returning to school at this time.

name: Urban Rooftop Gardening (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This Middle School service requires a Grade 11 coach-mentor with relevant experience and practical skills. In addition, they should have the interpersonal skills to motivate and support 11-13 year olds. Applicants will be interviewed to assess their suitability for the position. The group will be responsible for creating and maintaining a rooftop garden at St John's St Margaret's Nursing Home (on Dover Ave, near our campus). Mainly edible plants will be grown. The garden will be enjoyed by nursing home residents and later will become a focus for interactions between those seniors and children from the kindergarten on the same site. The core activities are very practical, but ultimately the service has both a social and sustainable development objective, aiming to show that good food can be grown close to home and without damaging the environment. The Grade 11 coach-mentor must be a "hands on" role model for the younger students, but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objectives of personal wellbeing (greenery is good for the mind and body) and a healthy environment. The coach-mentor's role will include running occasional Reflection meetings. The student may occasionally be required to stay past 4:30pm in order to review the session with the service coach-mentor.

name: UWCSEA Forever (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger students. Interested students will be interviewed by the service coach-mentors. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal and practical commitment to sustainability, as well as relevant coach-mentorship and communications skills for motivating and guiding other students. YOU MUST BE ABLE TO USE A SEWING MACHINE WITH REASONABLE PROFICIENCY and be able to teach others. The service aims to reduce consumption by transforming used uniforms (and possibly other materials) into useful items such as whiteboard erasers, bottle holders and more. The G11 coach-mentor must care about the problem of waste, be creative and have good practical and organisational skills. They will be expected to lead investigations into what happens to preloved clothes in Singapore and design projects to upcycle preloved uniforms into useful items, such as luggage tags and lunchbox bags.

name: St Andrew's Cathedral Home (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: NB: This is a leadership position that is suitable only for a very responsible student who can coach and mentor younger (MS) students. Applicants will be interviewed by the service leader or Head of Service.The home is in Clementi and the residents are 20-25 elderly women. It is important to understand that this is a Middle School service and thus the Grade 11 students main role is to mentor younger students and be a role model for them. The students and their Service Leader will plan activities such as Bingo, simple ball throwing, party games, card-making and art/craft. Grade 11 students should encourage the younger students to interact with the residents rather than simply focus on the activity. (Even without a shared verbal language, it is possible to communicate through eye contact and gestures.) Grade 11s must also lead a reflection after the activity and help the MS students plan for the following week. Chinese language skills would be useful but are not essential.

name: Genesis Special Needs Sports Coaching Service (G11)
description: Teaching basic sports skills to intellectually disabled students from Genesis School for Special Education. A key aim is to develop motor skills. It is important for UWCSEA students to understand that their own sporting skills are not very important. Instead, qualities such as care and resilience and skills such as creativity and communication are essential for Special Sports service. You must get to know your Genesis buddy well and plan your coaching to match his/her needs and potential ability.

name: LIONS Befrienders Active Ageing Centre (Mei Ling St)
description: Befriending seniors at the active ageing centre and planning activities such as checkers, chess, Jenga, Connect-4, jigsaws, Art & Craft, painting, gentle dance or sitting yoga. It is important to investigate what the seniors' interests are and keep centre staff informed of your plans. The Lions staff have stressed that they want students to show creativity and dynamism in planning activities!

name: Lakeside Reading (Jurong West)
description: A variety of reading and learning activities with children aged 6-10 from mostly low income homes. Students do "paired reading" with a child, supporting their reading comprehension and recording their progress. During the local school holidays, the children often come to our campus for planned activities. UWCSEA students need to be sensitive communicators, adapting their language to suit the comprehension level of their "buddy". They should also aim to motivate their partner and encourage enthusiasm for books. A relaxed, "anything goes" approach will not succeed as some children need firm guidance and good role modelling. The service group includes Grade 9, 10 and 11 students.

name: Institute of Mental Health (Teenage Unit)
description: Student visits to the IMH give patients an opportunity to develop social skills and build relationships with people other than their therapists and family members. You will work in the Teenage unit of the hospital, chatting, playing board games etc according to the interests and therapeutic needs of the patients. Numbers of patients and their needs vary from week to week. The service calls for a calm, open approach and sometimes a degree of resilience.

name: St. Theresa's Home (Mon)
description: Residential home for the elderly. The exact nature of the weekly activities will depend on requests from the residents and staff. However, students should prioritise personal interaction and sensitive listening: do things WITH people, not TO them ... In the past, St Theresa's staff have asked students to plan a series of activities spanning about 5 weeks: e.g. a simulated "coffee shop" with real refreshments; board games; Christmas concert; a walk in the grounds; Balloon Basketball. It is important to seek feedback from the seniors and the centre staff so that the service can be constantly improved. Speakers of Mandarin Chinese, Hokkien and Malay are especially welcome.

name: Anglican Care Centre Music Therapy
description: Students cannot sign up directly for this service as you must have an interview with Ms Newman to ensure you have suitable music skills and enthusiasm. Please sign up for an interview viathisdocument. Interested students can also contact Ms Sally Newman directly (Music Department):scn@uwcsea.edu.sg.This is an immensely rewarding service focused on providing fun and engaging musical (or other artistic) activities for adults with mental illness. Research shows that music can help alleviate various mental health conditions, including depression, trauma, and schizophrenia. Music acts as a medium for processing emotions, trauma, and grief. It can also be used to help calm anxiety.The Anglican Care Centre (ACC), Bukit Batok provides psychiatric rehabilitation and counselling services to adults with mental illness. These are people who live in their own homes but have generally not been able to hold down a job due to their illness. However, many belong to "work units" within the centre, getting a small allowance for cleaning, food preparation, administrative tasks etc. Every ACC client has a personal counsellor and counsellors are always on duty at the centre.There are around 20 regular clients. The clients travel to our campus and meet in a Music room. It is important to operate in an adaptive way, changing plans to match better with the nature of the individuals participating. The service may be challenging at times but over the course of the year you will develop your empathy, patience, boost your communication skills, and expand your understanding of what it means to be human.Additional InformationFor additional information, please go tohttps://www.aic.sg/body-mind/about-mental-health-conditions

name: Big Heart Student Care Centre (Qifa PS)
description: Big Heart is a non-profit organisation that offers before and after-school care services to students aged 7 to 12 years old. This particular centre is at Qifa Primary School, West Coast. The majority of the children are from socially disadvantaged families, where typically both parents are working long hours for low pay. UWCSEA students are encouraged to organise enrichment activities, including drama, voice projection, games, quizzes, music and art/craft. Any student care centre service provides a great opportunity to achieve CAS learning outcomes by showing creativity, planning activities, developing new skills etc. It is important to engage in a collaborative relationship with Big Heart staff, sharing plans with them and evaluating the success of activities together. It is equally important to be an effective communicator and mentor, responding sensitively to the children's needs and interests. This might involve adapting your language and perhaps adopting a firmer "teacher-like" role than you are used to!

name: Big Heart Student Care Centre (Princess Elizabeth PS, Bukit Batok)
description: Big Heart is a non-profit organisation that offers before and after-school care services to students aged 7 to 12 years old. This particular centre is at Princess Elizabeth Primary School, Bukit Batok West Avenue 3. The majority of the children are from socially disadvantaged families, where typically both parents are working long hours for low pay. UWCSEA students are encouraged to organise enrichment activities, including drama, voice projection, games, quizzes, music and art/craft. name: St. Luke's Hospital
description: St Luke's is a rehabilitation hospital for elderly patients who have recently had operations in government hospitals. Most stay for only a few weeks before returning home or moving to a nursing home. Students are expected to go to patients' bedsides and initiate conversations (where welcome). Most students find that conversation alone is sufficient to fill the hour. However, others may find that a patient enjoys playing a board game, Bingo (with fellow patients), a quiz such as "Guess the Singapore landmark", "Guess the Drawing" or "Guess the Price", or even a spot of karaoke! The group will need to discuss what is appropriate with the hospital staff. (Be prepared to try activities and sometimes fail.)

name: Clay Play service (G9 Coach-Mentor) (S1 - 2 / Sep start)
description: This is a selective Coach-Mentor service. Shortlisted students will be interviewed by the staff leader, but only one place will be allocated.A Grade 9 or 10 student is needed to assist two Primary School teachers in the Clay Play activity. This is an activity for Grade 4 and 5 students. It would be helpful if you have experience of ceramics, but most importantly you need to be able to mentor younger students. This includes emphasising safety and responsible handling of materials. Good communication skills and a supportive approach are essential.

name: Clay Play service (G9 Coach-Mentor) (S3 - 4 / Feb start)
description: This is a selective Coach-Mentor service. name: CSB For Refugees Academic Mentoring (Mon)
description: This service is only suitable for students who are confident about teaching IGCSE Mathematics, Science (Physics, Chemistry and Biology) and/or Computer Science. (Some English language mentoring will be required at times also.) You MUST have a good wifi connection to do the service. Be aware that your camera must be switched on throughout the session.In 2017, the Myanmar army attacked the Rohingya & Myanmar Muslims, causing hundreds of thousands to flee their homes. Cahaya Surya Bakti (CSB) students are among the refugees who reached Malaysia. Those in their teens aspire to gain IGCSE qualifications and get the opportunity to study in the Philippines, where four CSB graduates are already at university. CSB is also approaching private Malaysian and Online universities, who may also accept refugee students, and offer scholarships. The CSB school operates with a mixture of full-time and volunteer teachers. Students prepare for IGCSE exams in ESL, Maths, Malay Language, Coordinated Science, and Computer Science. Lessons will be conducted in Google Meet chatrooms. Students will need to be competent in the relevant subject, but also clear in their communication and capable of planning lessons that fit the students current knowledge and abilities.

description: This service is only suitable for students who are confident about teaching IGCSE Mathematics, Science (Physics, Chemistry and Biology) and/or Computer Science. name: LIONS Befrienders Active Ageing Centre (Ghim Moh)
description: An "active ageing" centre for elderly people from the Ghim Moh neighbourhood. Students plan different activities from week to week. These might include board games (e.g. checkers, chess, Jenga, Connect-4, jigsaws, backgammon); Bingo; art & craft; nail painting; gentle dance or sitting yoga and karaoke. It is important to investigate what the seniors' interests are and keep centre staff informed of your plans. The Lions staff have stressed that they want students to show creativity and dynamism in planning activities - "We want activities that upskill the seniors - not just Bingo and board games!"

name: Red Cross Home for the Disabled
description: Bedside interaction with adult residents who have severe physical and intellectual disabilities. Students are likely to engage with one patient (one on one).The essence of the service is to show compassion, but in practical terms students might do finger puppetry, prepare simple craft activities (not colouring in), read a picture book, do hand massage, sing songs etc. After 4:15, students can help feed a resident.

name: Peacebuilders (IFP for Grades 9 - 10)
description: A structured series of workshops on how to promote peace in our personal relationships and in the communities in which we belong. Among the themes will be equity, inclusion and justice.

name: St John's St Margaret's Nursing Home (SJSM) (G11 Coach Mentor)
description: A Middle School service for which students will organise games and engaging activities to help improve the quality of life for nursing home seniors. The home is on Dover Avenue, close to our campus, and the group will walk over there. The G11 coach-mentor (selected via an interview process) will be responsible for managing occasional planning sessions, motivating the younger students, acting as a fully engaged role model, and consulting effectively with SJSM staff and the seniors themselves to ensure that our activities and interaction are appropriate.

name: Boarding Buddies Homework Club S1/S2
description: G9 and 10 boarders help Junior School students develop effective study and time management habits. This requires the ability to motivate students and to model effective ways to develop better focus and study strategies. Once homework is completed students then play with students.

name: Infant Classroom Support / Monday (Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student. Interested students will be interviewed by Ms Emily Nocita, Grade Curriculum Coordinator. The role is only suitable for students who have a proven ability to work effectively with early years children, as well as the potential to lead a team of Grade 9/10 students doing service in Infant School classrooms. The selected student will need to help at an Investigation and Preparation session for students before they begin their service; work alongside students in different classrooms, coaching them as necessary; keep a record of G9/10 attendance that is shared with classroom teachers; and participate in scheduled reflection meetings with Ms Strachan. The Grade 11 coach-mentor will be responsible for approx. eight classrooms. They will have a schedule for spending a week at a time in each classroom, supporting the G9/FIB student there. This will require sensitive observation, role modelling and conversations that boost the younger student's capacity to be effective as a classroom support person.

name: Noh Bo Academy (online English for refugees) Mon (B)
description: Note that this service takes place at 7:00-8:00pm.Online English language tuition for refugees from Myanmar. One-on-one (mostly) conversation and teaching of English language skills with secondary school students forced to flee Myanmar due to the armed conflict there. Many of the students have a good level of previous education, but they need to boost their English language speaking and literacy to stand a chance of being accepted by overseas universities. A UWCSEA staff member will oversee each weekly session, welcoming students to a Google Meet and then assigning them to chat rooms for the personalised tuition. This service requires a high degree of responsibility and compassion as our partners have experienced recent trauma and have high expectations of the opportunity provided by the service.

name: Campus Biodiversity (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for a Grade 11 student who will coach G9/10 students doing the service. Campus Biodiversity is a hands-on environmental service project dedicated to protecting and improving the school s natural habitats. Our work includes surveying and recording campus wildlife, maintaining dragonfly ponds through weekly cleaning and water quality testing, monitoring ecological changes, planting native species and a lot more. We also support bird conservation by setting up artificial nesting boxes for species like hornbills and owls, while engaging the school community in hands-on activities to promote biodiversity awareness. The coach-mentor must have relevant knowledge and communication skills.

name: Incredible Edible: Herb Garden (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger (MS) students. Interested students will be interviewed. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to sustainable development through previous action or service learning. The selected students will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Grade 9-10 Incredible Edible groups. The core activities are very practical - propagating edible herbs in the nursery, sowing seeds and planting cuttings. You will also remove weeds from planting spaces, prepare the soil, plant out seedlings, and do mulching and watering as necessary. The Grade 11 coach-mentors must be a "hands on" role model but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objective of a sustainable future. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings.

name: Second Life (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger (MS) students. Interested students will be interviewed. To be considered, you must show evidence of a personal commitment to sustainable development through previous action or service learning. The selected coach-mentor must be a "hands on" role model but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objective of a sustainable future. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings.Second Life manages the collection, sorting and redistribution of "pre-loved" items from the UWCSEA community. On a weekly basis, students will be involved in hands-on chores, categorising, moving and storing both small and large items - everything from tiny figurines or Legos to quite large furniture and electronic items. Only join us if you are prepared to roll your sleeves up! The coach-mentor's role may include meeting with various school departments to arrange pick-ups and check on any planned 'retirement' of goods currently in service.

name: Feed the Earth - Composting & Mulching Service (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger students doing the service. Interested students will be interviewed by the service leaders. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal and practical commitment to sustainability, as well as relevant coach-mentorship and communications skills for motivating and guiding other students. Most Singapore soil is not suitable for successful gardening or tree planting. It needs to be enriched with compost, which is basically plant food in the form of decomposed vegetables, fruits, leaves etc. Students in this service (both Middle School and Grades 9/10) will do the practical chores that turn food waste, leaves etc into rich soil for the College gardens and endangered tree species. They will also be responsible for transporting compost by wheelbarrow to trees and gardens all over the campus and spreading this "mulch" around the plants. They may also be required to help occasionally with the wormery projects by cutting up salad items and leaves. The Grade 11 coach-mentor will be expected to do all the practical chores, but also to deepen their understanding of the composting process through research, observation of experts and attendance at any available workshops, subsequently sharing their knowledge and skills with the group. In summary, the student coach-mentor must be committed, enthusiastic and a good communicator.

name: Jakarta Street Kids GC (HS)
description: Jakarta Street Kids (JSK) Global Concern is focused on helping underprivileged children living in and around Jakarta, Indonesia. These children live in extremely vulnerable circumstances, many of whom are forced to live and work on the streets.Working together with our collaborating NGO XSProject Indonesia, we aspire to improve the lives of families living in poverty in Jakarta s trash picker communities. Specifically, we aim to promote the sales of XSProject merchandise within the UWCSEA community. Using discarded materials such as billboards, banners and plastic packaging, XSProject create fun, functional and upcycled products that find a new life with consumers and create social awareness about the effects of waste.In collaboration with our second partner organisation Yayasan Usaha Mulia (YUM), we aim to raise awareness and funds in support of education, health and community initiatives. Jakarta Street Kids supports educational sponsorships and holistic family based services for children living in Cipanas, West Java, near Jakarta.

name: SurfAid
description: SurfAid helps to improve health and education in isolated regions in Indonesia.

name: Tiger Watch
description: To support tiger conservation efforts through Harimau Kita's work with the Sumatran Tiger.

name: Amala
description: Amala provides transformative education programmes for refugee/displaced learners who want to make a change. We support the programmes in Kakuma, Kenya.

name: Cahaya Surya Bakti (CSB)
description: Cahaya Surya Bakti GC (CSB) is a dynamic grassroots organisation providing community support and education for refugees (primarily Rohingya from Myanmar) in Malaysia.

name: Pakistan
description: To support the work of The Citizen Foundation in their objective to provide quality education to Pakistani children.

name: Pinoy
description: To promote educational opportunities for marginalized communities in the Philippines through Lihuk

name: LEAF
description: To promote good environmental practices on campus.

name: Promoting Animal Welfare (PAW)
description: PAW is committed to raising the awareness of animal rights, welfare, protection and conservation. PAW has several links with organisations in Singapore (ACRES and SPCA) and the region.

name: Rainforest
description: To be actively involved in projects both on campus and in Singapore. To actively engage in growing and planting indigenous trees in Singapore.

name: UWCSEA Rainforest Restoration: Tree Nursery Skills (Thu) (Sep start)
description: The UWCSEA Rainforest Restoration Project collaborates with local partners to help boost Singapore's tree cover and spread awareness of the importance of trees for the ecosystem, society, economy and personal wellbeing. You will help raise indigenous trees from seed and cuttings. You will also help with essential maintenance tasks such as watering, mulching and weeding. On alternate weeks (weather permitting) you will go off campus to the Rail Corridor behind Commonwealth MRT station in order to do maintenance work on saplings planted there. Please be aware that on those days, the service will run for longer due to travel time: you will normally be able to go to Commonwealth MRT station around 4:30/4:40 or take a bus back to school, arriving around 4:50pm.

name: St Joseph's Home
description: UWCSEA students have been visiting this home for over 35 years. The objective is to befriend a few elderly residents of a 300-bed home through conversation, cards, board games etc. An ability to speak Chinese isveryuseful, though it's not essential. Take the time to get to know your St Joseph's friend and respond to his/her interests. A loud voice and good communication skills (especially listening) in one to one conversations are very important.

name: Guild Buddies (Thursday)
description: The Guild is a small international "college" for teenagers with special needs. These are students with multiple disabilities, such as autism, severe ADHD, Down Syndrome and cerebral palsy. The primary aim of the service interaction is to build social skills through regular interaction with peers who do not have disabilities. Suitable activities might include structured play, dance or drama, but it is your responsibility as a service group to investigate the students' interests and abilities, speaking with both them and their teachers in order to choose and trial a variety of activities.This video gives you a flavour of the service: https://drive.google.com/file/d/188CanM2LE3JGW7ws1UTqxqOpPUSYSicb/view?usp=drive_link

name: Child at Street 11
description: Child at Street 11 is an early childhood centre in Ang Mo Kio. The majority of the children come from low income families and, without the centre, would miss out on the kind of preschool experience that most Singaporean children have. The centre adopts a Reggio Emilia inspired learning approach that helps the children become self-reliant, confident, and emotionally resilient. The UWCSEA service group plans and runs activities for a particular small class. These might include shared reading, art or basic writing on a particular theme, structured play and various games. The service leader is a very experienced early childhood teacher. Students doing the service must feel comfortable working with small children, have the necessary patience and empathy, be ready to adapt their language, and have a real desire to support the children's learning and language development.

name: Incredible Edible (Thursday) (Sep start)
description: Raising edible plants in green spaces on campus. Your team will propagate plants in the nursery, sowing seeds and planting cuttings that will later become edible plants. You will also remove weeds from planting spaces, prepare the soil, plant out seedlings, and do mulching and watering as necessary.

name: HS Green Campus (Sep start)
description: Help us achieve our goal of making the campus more sustainable. You will work with other students on various tasks such as classroom paper recycling, blue bin management, monitoring of eWaste bins and other waste reduction activities. Students may also work on other projects to promote sustainability on campus.

name: Zero Waste (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger students doing the Zero Waste service. Interested students will be interviewed by the service leader. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal and practical commitment to sustainability, as well as relevant coaching and communications skills for motivating and guiding other students. The selected student will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Zero Waste group. The service aims to reduce consumption by finding a second or third life for pre-owned items. The group will investigate ways to collect unwanted items from the UWCSEA community and the College's Lost and Found store, and organise an occasional "Zero Waste Day" to redistribute the items. The student coach-mentor must care about the problem of waste and be ready to work collaboratively and strategically to come up with effective solutions. To be successful, the group will need to work with relevant staff and student groups, such as the Second Life service (MS), Repair Workshop and LEAF GC.

name: Magical Murals (Sep start)
description: Help plan and paint murals to brighten up various spaces around the campus. Many of the murals done so far have been created to represent our UWCSEA values. You can see several examples on the 2nd floor, outside the Wellness Centre and opposite the College Shop. Begin by investigating what is possible and preferred: interview relevant people, such as the Head of Facilities and GC groups. Plan your designs in teams for specific purposes. Get painting. Your service leader is an Art teacher! Note that this unit runs for two seasons, though an extension may be possible. Only suitable for students who have chosen to do Art in Grade 9/10.

name: Here2Hear
description: Here2Hear is a year-long student service group dedicated to supporting mental health and promoting wellness through meaningful peer connections. As a member, you ll help raise awareness, support fellow students one-on-one, and lead campus initiatives that foster a more caring and connected school community. In addition to facilitating the group s service initiatives which include activities that promote wellbeing and belonging around campus you ll also be expected to contribute some time outside of Friday lunches by supporting other service events and programs (such as school tours for new students, etc).You ll also receive specialized training in peer counseling, active listening, and leadership skills that are valuable for life and especially relevant to careers in psychology, healthcare, law, business, and education. This is a great opportunity to develop your communication and emotional intelligence while making a real difference.With a maximum of 20 members per year, Here2Hear offers a close-knit, purposeful experience. While membership is renewed annually, we encourage students to continue membership in the service group throughout their high school journey. If you re interested in joining (and did not complete Peer Support Training last academic year), please submit a short letter of intent to the supervisors. This can be a written letter, an audio or video recording of yourself, or shared through an informal interview with a supervisor. In your letter, let us know:- Why you re interested in joining;- What Service means to you;- Why you think Here2Hear will be an important group in our school community.We re looking for thoughtful, empathetic students who want to lead through listening and help create a school where every voice is heard. We re Here2Hear.

name: New Life Student Care Centre
description: Student Care Centres (SCCs) keep children safe and positively occupied after their regular school day finishes. In most cases, their parents are still out at work. The SCCs provide a mixture of homework supervision and enrichment/recreational activities. The New Life SCC webpages states: "We are more than just a before-and-after school centre: we mentor children to become disciplined and confident individuals. Over the years, we have built a name for ourselves with the vibrant, holistic programmes that we provide and the nurturing and caring teacher-mentors in our centres. [...] Through the students relationship with their teacher-mentors as well as the character-based programmes we run, they will be moulded into wholesome, confident individuals and contribute to society."For this service, the SCC children travel from the Teck Whye neighbourhood to our campus. The UWCSEA student group is expected to plan suitable activities (eg. board games, quizzes, ball games, crafts). It is important to collaborate with the New Life staff. You should make use of our campus spaces to match the interests and needs of the New Life children. IDEAS Hub? Science labs? Gardens? David Watson Library? Most things are possible if you ask the right people. Weather permitting, you might finish your session in the Adventure Playground.

name: Campus Gardening (Sep start)
description: Help boost personal wellbeing on the campus by working for our Campus Gardening project. Your team will propagate plants in the nursery, sowing seeds and planting cuttings that will later become the flowers and shrubs we need to beautify our garden spaces. You will also remove weeds from planting spaces, prepare the soil, plant out flowers and shrubs, and do mulching and watering as necessary. A hands-on project that should prove therapeutic after a day in the classroom!

name: Guild Buddies (Tuesday)
description: The Guild is a small international "college" for teenagers with special needs. Around sixteen students will visit our campus each week. These are students with multiple disabilities, such as autism, severe ADHD, Down Syndrome and cerebral palsy. The primary aim of the service interaction is to build social skills through regular interaction with peers who do not have disabilities. Suitable activities might include structured play, dance or drama, but it is your responsibility as a service group to investigate the students' interests and abilities, speaking with both them and their teachers in order to choose and trial a variety of activities.This video gives you a flavour of the service: https://drive.google.com/file/d/188CanM2LE3JGW7ws1UTqxqOpPUSYSicb/view?usp=drive_link

name: Cheshire Home (Thursday)
description: Conversation, board games etc with adult residents who have various physical disabilities.

name: Magical Murals (Feb start)
description: Help plan and paint murals to brighten up various spaces around the campus. Many of the murals done so far have been created to represent our UWCSEA values. You can see several examples on the 2nd floor, outside the Wellness Centre and opposite the College Shop. Begin by investigating what is possible and preferred: interview relevant people, such as the Head of Facilities and GC groups. Plan your designs in teams for specific purposes. Get painting. Your service leader is an Art teacher! Note that this unit runs for two seasons, though an extension may be possible. Only suitable for students who have chosen to do Art in Grade 9/10.

name: National University Hospital (NUH)
description: Both students and staff leader must have documentary evidence of up-to-date vaccinations against the following infections. (The hospital will request documentation on your first visit, so you should not sign up for NUH if you know you will be unable to supply the necessary evidence.)1. COVID-19 (+ Booster)2. MMR (Measles-Mumps-Rubella)3. Tetanus4. Diptheria5. Pertussis (whooping cough)6. Varicella (chickenpox)The National University Hospital (NUH) is a major teaching hospital. This often makes the service attractive to students who intend to apply for medical courses. However, a word of warning: your service will be nothing like an internship and you will have no opportunity at all to work alongside medical staff. You would probably gain more relevant experience in a nursing home, where at least you would meet the same residents every week.Students in the NUH service interact with patients in either a children s ward or a ward for teenage/adult patients with mental illness. You will need a friendly face, reasonable conversation skills and a good sense of humour. The most common activity in the psychiatric ward is a card or board game, but it is important to remember that the game itself is far less important than the social experience your aim is to affirm your partner's sense of self-worth and join them in a calming activity. On the children's wards, it is often challenging to find a child who wants to play as they often prefer to cling to the security of a parent or carer who is at their bed. Your task is to find cunning ways to entice the child into the play area and join you in a game. If you are naturally shy or introverted, you will need to overcome that and become an entertainer. You will get a chance to work on both kinds of ward, probably switching mid-year.

name: St. John's - St. Margaret's Nursing Home
description: St John's St Margaret's Nursing Home opened in 2021. It is situated on Dover Avenue, opposite the UWCSEA campus, and we make our weekly visits on foot. The home has over 250 residents and includes a Senior Activity Centre for non-residents. Students plan activities that match with the residents' interests and social or cognitive needs; the overall aim is to provide the seniors with a positive social experience that enhances their wellbeing. Examples of activities include word and number games, "Old Singapore" quizzes, music and movement activities, art & craft, board games, magic tricks, and Bingo. Students should be imaginative in their planning: for example, a game such as Twister can be adapted for seniors by requiring them to throw bean bags onto the mat rather than physically twisting their bodies!In the longer run, SJSM staff hope to have an intergenerational "village" called Project Spring-Winter, in which kindergarten children and seniors will spend parts of the day together. However, this project has been delayed by COVID-19 restrictions.

name: Jamiyah Nursing Home (Grade 11)
description: NOTE: Students must be able to show documentary evidence (or the Trace Together phone app) to prove that they are fully vaccinated against COVID-19. You must also have evidence ready (in case requested) to show that you are vaccinated against MMR, Chickenpox and Pertussis.A nursing home with mainly middle-aged to elderly residents. The majority are Malay speakers. Students converse, play board games, do manicures etc. (We have nail varnish etc in the Service office.) It is important to get to know the residents and explore what their interests are so that you can plan suitable activities. Be inventive: for example, people in wheelchairs can play Twister by throwing bean-bags onto the mat. It would be easy to play a board game every week, but so much more fun to try new things. This year, for the first time, the home has requested basic digital literacy training for a small group of seniors. IPads will be available and the seniors will want to learn how to connect with relatives via a video app such as Zoom (very useful in case sections of the home are ever isolated due to a COVID outbreak). They will also be interested to learn how to take photos, watch Youtube videos and play simple games.The service group includes both Grade 11 and Grade 9/10 students.

name: Ren Ci Nursing Home @ AMK
description: The home caters for patients who cannot afford the fees charged by more expensive hospitals and homes, and many are long-term patients who are unlikely to return home. Students befriend these patients and try to develop a special relationship with one or more of them. Not many speak English, so students who can speak Hokkien, Mandarin or Malay have an advantage; however, this is not a requirement. There are games, etc. in the Service Office that some of the residents would enjoy, and students could also try hand massage. Listening is probably the most important skill in this service. Don t fall into a rut of Bingo or whatever week after week be creative, take risks, learn

name: Ren Ci Nursing Home @ Bukit Batok Street 52
description: Board games, crafts, table tennis, hand massage etc with elderly nursing home residents. It is important to get to know individual residents and learn about their interests in order to plan suitable activities. Be prepared to experiment with new things. For example, the roof garden and ground floor function room are both available for activities (the function room even has a set of drums and a ping-pong table). The home caters for patients who cannot afford the fees charged by more expensive hospitals and homes, and many are long-term patients who are unlikely to return home.

name: New Life Student Care Centre (Sep start)
description: Student Care Centres (SCCs) keep primary school children safe and positively occupied after their regular school day finishes. In most cases, their parents are still out at work. The SCCs provide a mixture of homework supervision and enrichment/recreational activities. The New Life SCC webpages states: "We are more than just a before-and-after school centre: we mentor children to become disciplined and confident individuals. Over the years, we have built a name for ourselves with the vibrant, holistic programmes that we provide and the nurturing and caring teacher-mentors in our centres. [...] Through the students relationship with their teacher-mentors as well as the character-based programmes we run, they will be moulded into wholesome, confident individuals and contribute to society."For this service, the SCC children travel from the Teck Whye neighbourhood to our campus. The UWCSEA student group is expected to plan suitable activities (eg. board games, quizzes, ball games, crafts). It is important to collaborate with the New Life staff. You should make use of our campus spaces to match the interests and needs of the New Life children. IDEAS Hub? Science labs? Gardens? David Watson Library? Most things are possible if you ask the right people. Weather permitting, you might finish your session in the Adventure Playground.

name: Green Campus Coach-Mentor
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger students doing the Green Campus service. Interested students will be interviewed. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to sustainable development through previous action or service learning. The selected students will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Grade 9-10 Green Campus groups. The core activities are very practical - especially collecting and sorting paper, electrical waste etc for recycling - but ultimately the groups aim to promote a culture of reduced consumption and waste in our community. The Grade 11 coach-mentors must be "hands on" role models for the younger students, but should also act as mentors and educators, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objective of a sustainable future. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings.

name: HER Journey (G11)
description: Students must be prepared to attend occasional events with MDWs on Sundays.HER Journey is a Migrant Domestic Worker (MDW) skills training and advocacy group. Past workshops have focussed on: Canva website building; Capcut video production; MS Word; MS Excel; Gmail; and public speaking. In the coming year, HER Journey s main focus will continue to be on practical skills lessons that improve the participants employability and self-confidence.HER Journey is looking for students who truly care about MDWs and are willing to devote both time and skills to supporting them in appropriate ways. This is an opportunity to develop coach-mentorship and interpersonal skills. In particular, we are looking for students who are imaginative and collaborative, as we are constantly thinking of ways we can better support MDWs. Still, we are looking for genuine commitment and willingness to learn rather than relevant experience.

name: Campus Gardening (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach Grade 9/10 students doing the Campus Gardening service. Interested students will be interviewed by the service coach-mentor. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to environmental conservation through previous action or service learning. Ideally, you would have practical gardening experience. The selected student will be expected to support the teacher who leads the service, preparing garden beds, planting, watering, weeding, improving soil etc. The core activities are very practical, but ultimately our Campus Gardening groups have community wellbeing and carbon capture objectives. The Grade 11 coach-mentor must be a "hands on" role model for the younger students, but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger sustainability objectives. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings. The student may occasionally be required to stay past 4:30pm in order to review the session with the service coach-mentor.All G11 coach-mentors of sustainability services are expected to meet at least twice a term with the Environmental Initiatives Council to report on progress and learn about opportunities for collaboration with other services and specific initiatives.

name: Big Heart Student Care Centre (Zhangde PS)
description: Big Heart is a non-profit organisation that offers before and after-school care services to students aged 7 to 12 years old. This particular centre is at Zhangde Primary School, Bukit Merah. The majority of the children are from socially disadvantaged families, where typically both parents are working long hours for low pay. UWCSEA students are encouraged to organise enrichment activities, including drama, voice projection, games, quizzes, music and art/craft. Any student care centre service provides a great opportunity to achieve CAS learning outcomes by showing creativity, planning activities, developing new skills etc. It is important to engage in a collaborative relationship with Big Heart staff, sharing plans with them and evaluating the success of activities together. It is equally important to be an effective communicator and mentor, responding sensitively to the children's needs and interests. This might involve adapting your language and perhaps adopting a firmer "teacher-like" role than you are used to!

name: Hannah Active Ageing Centre
description: Hannah, like other Active Ageing Centres, provides daytime activities for seniors who live at home but welcome the social, cognitive and even physical stimulation that the centre can offer. Students will need to approach the service with open minds, prepared to both teach and learn. Find out what the seniors' interests are and do not impose unwelcome activities on them. You may find that board games are popular, but the Hannah seniors like to try new things, so you must be creative. In 2023, our group began collaborating with seniors on a Life Stories project. This was not completed, but you may find that the centre manager and some seniors are interested in reviving it.Read more about similar projects here:https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GIR9IfJv69E&ab_channel=SPRUvideos

name: Anglican Care Centre - Games, Sport, Art & Music (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: Students cannot sign up directly for this service; you must be interviewed by a staff member.The Anglican Care Centre (ACC), Bukit Batok provides psychiatric rehabilitation and counselling services to adults with mental illness. These are people who live in their own homes but have generally not been able to hold down a job due to their illness. However, many belong to "work units" within the centre, getting a small allowance for cleaning, food preparation, administrative tasks etc. Every ACC client has a personal counsellor and counsellors are always on duty at the centre.This is a Middle School (Grade 7-8) service that requires one Grade 11 coach-mentor. The coach-mentor's role is to help the younger students plan and run the activities effectively. There are around 20 regular ACC clients. The clients travel to our campus and meet in a classroom or activity space. It is important to operate in an adaptive way, changing plans to match better with the nature of the individuals participating.

name: Christina Noble Children's Foundation (CNCF)
description: To help the children's home and street children by providing material goods and through a sponsorship scheme.

name: Empowering the Children of Vietnam (ECOV) / Blue Dragon

This programme is aimed at students who want to learn the basics and advanced techniques of beach volleyball, whether it is to prepare for competition or for the fun of beach volleyball. Through this programme, we encourage our students to lead an active and healthy lifestyle and to enjoy the sport.

By strengthening their public speaking skills, students gain confidence and qualify to represent a country delegation in an MUN committee or council during conferences.

name: Finance Society
description: This society aims to provide a brief introduction to the fundamentals of finance, emphasising their application to a wide variety of real-world situations spanning from personal finance, corporate decision-making and valuation. Key concepts include: time value of money, risk-return tradeoff, cost of capital, interest rates, retirement savings, mortgage financing, asset valuation, discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis, net present value, internal rate of return, hurdle rate, payback period.

description: The Dungeons and Dragons Club is for students to come together for some role playing and collaborative story telling.

name: Bio Society
description: Our society learns various things related to biology including, but not limited to, DNA analysis, astrobiology, bacteria culturing, and more. We also conduct practicals based on the society's interests, such as DNA extraction and dissections. We also plan to play games and create posters/infographics. We hope to see you there :)

name: Politics Society
description: This is the best of times; this is the worst of times. With regard to the current global political climate and even events within our own community, we believe that politics is something that we should all be able to understand. In Politics Society (PolSoc), we proliferate the ideas and concepts that revolve around politics as a way to contribute to the overall education of the UWC community. We will focus on discussing current events that occur around the world, educating students on the basics of political ideology, and linking these concepts to the present-day global political climate. Politics Society is all about open debate from all directions: Left, Right, Up, Down and Centre. Everyone is welcome to come to PolSoc, even if they haven t signed up on CIMS!

name: Ethical Hacking Society
description: A society to educate you on the limits and strengths of computers and their vulnerabilities. With opportunities to compete competitively in CTF competitions run by FANG+ organisations.

name: Business Society
description: Business Society is a society dedicated to fostering entrepreneurial spirit, where members engage in learning about how businesses work and how to successfully set up their own ventures.

name: Softball Skills Development (MS & HS)
description: The Softball Development Skill Session is for students ages 12 to 19 who want to improve their skills in preparation for competitive games. Training will focus on improving your areas of weakness in your respective skill departments in a much smaller group setting.

name: Japanese Magazine Society
description: In this activity, we will make a fantastic magazine in Japanese! We are going to discuss real-world issues or trending music, movie, and books. We will openly discuss them with members as well as have the responsibility to share them with people including UWC students, parents, and teachers through making magazines. Join us if you have a passion for the Japanese community and let's enjoy the journey of improving Japanese writing skills together!

name: Geography Society
description: Why do ancient civilisations appear where they are? Could we tackle climate change using mirrors? Geography is about more than looking at rocks and clouds. In GeoSoc we discuss real world issues and their implications on the present and future. It s a chance to debate, present on topics of your choice, have a leadership opportunity and much more! But most importantly, have fun interacting and learning about all things Geography!

name: Initiative for Peace (IFP)
description: Initiative for Peace (IFP) invites all Grade 11's interested to join and experience an intensive programme covering leadership and facilitation skills. The programme also deals with the theoretical aspects needed to gain an understanding of what can lead to conflict or peace. IFP works towards building links between our students with youth from countries who are undergoing or have gone through conflict. The goal is to work together to achieve sustainable peace in their communities. You will be trained for a year and have the opportunity to be selected to plan one of three conferences: Timor Leste, Mae Sot and Peace Day. The first two of which run in the first week of the summer vacation. We hope you will join us and we look forward to seeing you. Keep an eye on the notices for the date, time and venue of our first session!

name: Psych Society
description: Everyone with an interest in finding out more about Psychology is welcome in PsychSoc. Whether you have no knowledge, a little knowledge or considerable knowledge. PsychSoc is a group for anyone interested in learning about Psychology or extending their knowledge of the subject. Grade 11 students have the opportunity to apply for a leadership position in seasons 2, 3 & 4 and present and lead activities on various interesting topics within psychology at the same time as developing their leadership skills.

description: Global Economics is a student-led club that focuses on the economics of nations. Students are able to develop their analysis, presentation, communication and problem solving skills by establishing a solid understanding of today's economic system across the globe. No prior knowledge concerning economics is required.

name: Epiphany Arts Platform
description: Epiphany is the HS student art community with six departments: Dance, Film & Theatre, Literature, Music, Photography and Visual Arts. Created artworks are displayed on our widely followed social media accounts and during our annual showcase, which has taken place in the form of an exhibition and an online magazine in the past years. There are also opportunities to collaborate with organisations in school to worldwide competitions and gallery launches. It is also counted as a CAS experience.

name: High School Percussion Ensemble
description: The High School Percussion Ensemble is for intermediate and advanced percussionists. Members of this group are asked to play the snare drum, bass drum, cymbals, xylophone, vibraphone and marimba at various times of the year. Students do not need to be advanced on any of the instruments but should be willing to learn how to play the instruments that they have not played before. For further information please contact Carl Jenkins jrcarlitp@gapps.uwcsea.edu.sg.

name: Chinese Culture - Chinese Calligraphy

name: Chess (Intermediate)
description: Unlike the season cycle of the Beginner Programme, this groups follows a more long-term approach in their development as a chess player. This is designed for individuals who have completed the beginner course, or those who possess a foundational understanding of the game's mechanics, this is also for those aspire to join the School Team but may not yet have the necessary skills.Additionally, this program caters to individuals who simply enjoy chess for its own sake and are not interested in competing or experiencing the pressure of competition, yet still aspire to master the game. Upon completion of the Intermediate program, participants will demonstrate a heightened understanding of advanced chess strategies and tactics, including but not limited to positional play, pawn structures, and various tactical motifs.

name: Chinese Literature & Culture Society
description: In this Club, students will read and share thoughts on the Chinese Literature book of their choice. They may also choose to write reviews or a piece of literature work based on their discussion.All levels are welcome!

name: Singers
description: 'Singers' is a High School (Grades 9 - 12) non-auditioned 4-part choir that rehearses and performs a wide range of musical styles and genres.While the student body will comprise the majority of members, Singers is now able to extend involvement to the wider UWC staff and parent community, creating a true community choir, particularly with a view to our finale during our 'Opus' event in March 2025 at the Yong Siew Toh Concert Hall.We meet on Monday lunchtime from 1pm - 1.40pm (alternative rehearsal time Friday 9:50 - 10:15 am in the event of an Monda6ECA clash)Please email should you be unable to rehearse at this time due to other ECA commitments. If you are committed, we will try to accommodate your availability with alternative catch up rehearsal times.There will be opportunities for solo as well as ensemble vocal roles.'Singers' provides excellent opportunities for all to participate creatively (involvement is very appropriate for CAS 'creativity') and no prior experience or vocal/ music reading ability is required or expected.Concert dates 2025/261)UWC Day:Friday, 19th September, lunchtime performance under the tent.2)TOCCATA2025: Saturday, 22nd November, 5.30pm, Main Hall. Dress rehearsals earlier in the day3)Opus 2026:Tuesday, 3rd March, 7:30 pm, Esplanade Concert Hall. Dress rehearsals Saturday 28th February and Sunday 1st March, Music Center.

name: Badminton Phoenix Squad
description: Badminton that provide tag environment all year round with our SEASAC 19A coaches, Destyan and Mandy.

name: Investment Society
description: The Investment Society has been set up by students who have a strong interest in investing and finance. We are keen to learn about stocks, stock markets and companies. The purpose of the society is to provide its members with an insight into investing. We will organise and invite guest speakers and debate and discuss various investment topics. We also plan to have a virtual investment portfolio, and several departments which will complete their own individual research. We are inviting students who have a similar interest and are who very willing to contribute.

name: Tennis development high school Friday
description: Group Coaching for beginner/intermediate level. Players will be placed into groups based on level on the first day. For more information regarding the programme go to the UWCSEA Dover Tennis Website, link below. Please contact dovertennis@uwcsea.edu.sg if you have any questions.

name: Astronomy Club
description: Have you ever had a spark of curiosity for the sky above you?Astro Club is a fun-filled activity which allows students to explore 'beyond Earth' topics such as the formation of the universe, the probability of alien existence, space technology, dark matter and blackholes. Join us on outings around Singapore using advanced telescopes to view celestial events (such as meteor showers, galaxies, nebulae and planets) that go beyond the regular school curriculum.We also offer the opportunity to join national competitions, on which we placed top 5 last year, competing with the likes of Raffles Institute, NUS High and Hwa Chong.Sign up now using CIMS or thislink.

name: Dover Today
description: Dover Today is the school's student-led newspaper. Founded in 2014 by Bennett Okun, Dover Today serves as a platform to amplify the voices of students and give them a space to share their views and opinions on a variety of topics. In this activity you'll get the chance to express your views and improve your writing skills in the process. We also offer weekly puzzles that are available to anyone viewing the website.

name: Tennis High school Friday
description: Group Coaching for beginner/intermediate level. Players will be placed into groups based on level on the first day. For more information regarding the programme go to the UWCSEA Dover Tennis Website, link below. Please contact dovertennis@uwcsea.edu.sg if you have any questions.

name: Don t Be Afraid Editorial Team
description: Don t Be Afraid UWC is a student run online magazine space that invites students to write about the pressing issues in our community and wider world. Are you interested in being part of the editorial team? This would include helping submitters to edit, coaching writers on how to improve their writing and helping shape discourse in our community.

name: Philosophy Debate Society
description: We discuss and debate popular philosophical topics, thought experiments and concepts by putting forth theories and evaluating ideas of philosophers in a structured socratic seminar format. Students will develop their debate and discussion skills as well as their logic skill and handle on complex philosophical ideas.

name: Diversity Equity Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) Student Group
description: The mission of the DEIJ student committee is to promote the principles of diversity, equity, inclusion and justice in order to fuel sustained positive change that fosters a safe and respectful space for all diverse identities to be well and thrive within our school community. We aim to act in solidarity with all marginalised identities and empower ourselves and others through cross-cultural dialogue as a way to find commonalities and celebrate our differences.This activity is an opportunity for students to develop cultural competence, partake in thought-provoking dialogue and develop numerous skills that will aid them both in and out of the classroom.If you have any questions, please email beaum103012@gapps.uwcsea.edu.sg

name: Visual Arts Magazine
description: An opportunity to support and promote young artists and the visual arts within our school's community. It is a safe and supportive environment for both artists and to work together to celebrate the visual arts.Collaborating with a team to put together an exciting, innovative and original Visual Arts magazine that celebrates the Visual Arts within UWCSEA Dover.

name: Black Student Union (BSU)
description: Black Student Union (BSU) invites all interested high school students to join and discuss some aspects of black culture. This activity also addresses theoretical and historical issues that affect the black diaspora. BSU's goal is to raise awareness about black culture in the community and to understand the global black struggle while taking particular heed to the fact that the black community is one of the smallest minorities in Singapore. We will engage in all sorts of activities like a newsletter, discussions and book recommendations. Looking forward to seeing you, all ideas help to enrich this activity!

name: Javanese Gamelan Ensemble
description: Come and learn to play our beautiful traditional Javanese Gamelan with a true Javanese Gamelan expert in Faezan Redwan! The first performance will be on Thursday October 9th in the Tent Plaza as part of the UN Night! No experience is required. Please contact Miles Tranter (mtr@gapps.uwcsea.edu.sg) if you have any further questions.

name: Chinese Mahjong Club
description: Students would learn how to play Chinese Mahjong, and understand the culture meaning behind it. The club also offers a perfect place for the students who already know how to play Mahjong to play with their friends. (Students will be allowed to bring their lunch to the classroom)

name: Music Performance Society
description: Welcome to the Music Performance Society - a student-led activity for music enthusiasts of all kinds, regardless of your musical background. Our aim is to create a vibrant, supportive music community through monthly sessions that blend performances and presentations.During performance sessions, you can showcase your musical talents by signing up via a Google Form with your chosen piece, no matter your skill level. The seasons concludes with an ensemble performance, and we will also host a charity concert annually to give back to the local community. Our presentation sessions offer a chance for you to share a music-related topic of your choice with an engaged audience. Join us to share, perform, and explore the world of music!

name: Music Composition Society
description: This is a student-led activity for composition enthusiasts. Our goal is to help students develop an appreciation for creating music as well as become more daring in their composition process. We will teach students new concepts and offer adequate support as they compose music in the session. In addition, students themselves may also share weekly concepts that interest them.

name: Economics and Business Internship
description: Calling G10 and G11 Economics, Business or Entrepreneurship students! Get ready for an incredible Business/Economics Department Internship. Imagine working in small groups alongside your peers, helping other students while creating awesome resources focused on business and economics. You'll be the ones making learning super engaging and fun! Plus, you'll get to collaborate with our awesome teachers to understand what students like you really need and help plan the curriculum. Sounds good? Wait, it gets even better! We've secured a designated space in the business/economics office just for you. You'll have your very own hub where you can work like professionals and collaborate in a safe and supportive environment. It's a chance to be treated as equals with the adults while having a blast together. Enjoyment aside, you'll also develop some seriously impressive skills. Think collaboration, leadership, creativity, and project management. You'll create educational materials, run workshops, and design interactive projects to help everyone understand the exciting world of business and economics. Together, we'll bring these concepts to life and make them super engaging for all students. It's a chance to showcase your innovative ideas and make a real impact on our school community. This internship is the ultimate opportunity to immerse yourself in the business and economics realm. We've designed it to be dynamic, empowering, and, most importantly, loads of fun! So, if you're ready for an adventure, submit an application for the Business/Economics Department Internship.

name: Law Society
description: Law Society is a student-led activity in which different areas of Law are discussed. Occasionally we have guest speakers, make visits to the courts in Singapore and have run a yearly mock trial.

name: Artificial Intelligence Society
description: Develop your skills in machine learning, computer vision, and natural language processing while collaborating with supportive peers. No prior AI / programming knowledge is required.

name: Forum Team
description: The Forum is a medium of civil discourse that amplifies student voices on the issues shaping our world. In our weekly discussions, held every Friday during mentor time, students are encouraged to share their perspectives with the support of guided questions on a central topic. Becoming a facilitator of Forum requires both facilitation skills, creative collaboration, and critical thinking. You must be able to commit to a weekly planning session conducted during break and the weekly Forum event.Selective (Pre-allocated - Students / Teachers will need to email the name list to the Activities Dept)

name: Boxing - Technical and Fitness
description: Boxing - Technical and Fitness is offering the opportunity to develop fundamental techniques and increase holistic fitness in a supportive and inclusive group setting.

name: Musical Rudiments
description: An individualised course in piano keyboard techniques/ musical theory/ compositional techniques for Middle and High School students. Students may opt for one or more of the above. If students wish, the courses may be designed to align with and work alongside existing examination boards (ABRSM or Trinity College) in practical performance and/ or theory (Grades 1 - 8).Season 1 and Season 2 there is no space for new students. Interested students/ parents may email @mfo@uwcsea.edu.sg from Season 3 onwards, if they are interested, when spaces become available.

name: Environmental Economics Society
description: Have you ever wondered how to solve environmental issues from a financial perspective? Environmental Economics aims to use financial markets and related regulations to deal with the most challenging environmental issues faced by the modern economy, such as pollution, global warming and environmental induced poverty. In this club, you will have access to real world cases of green finance through invited guest speakers from related industries and research institutions. You will also have opportunities to expand your knowledge and express your views through discussions and presentations. No prior knowledge is required. Join us!

name: Badminton (Small Group Training)
description: Our Badminton Programme is open to students of all skill levels, with a special focus on helping beginners build a solid foundation in the sport. Participants will learn essential skills such as basic strokes, serving techniques, and hitting drills that are important for gameplay.We understand that every child learns at their own pace and in their own way, whether through visual demonstration or conceptual understanding. To support this, each student will receive a progression card on the first day of training, enabling both the student and coach to track weekly learning milestones.Skills and strokes will be practised and refined until students can execute them consistently before moving on to more advanced techniques. This ensures they are able to perform their best shots during rallies. By the end of each session, students will have opportunities to rally with friends and schoolmates while gaining an understanding of the basic rules of badminton.The programme is led by Coach Mandy, an early childhood specialist passionate about introducing badminton to younger generations. Her goal is to inspire and nurture the next wave of players who may one day represent the school in their age group.

name: Med Soc
description: Medsoc is UWCSEA Dover's medical society where we discuss all topics related to medicine. This includes presentations, discussions, practical sessions, the medical school application process and your own interests. Dissections and practical sessions where members get hands-on experience with different medical equipment is also carried out.

name: The Economics Society
description: The Economics Society is a dynamic and engaging activity designed to foster a comprehensive understanding of economics and its real-world applications. We aim to provide an interactive platform where participants can explore economic concepts and global economic events through a series of presentations, discussions, and interactive activities. Leadership and presentation opportunities are available for participants to develop leadership and communication skills, strengthening individual ability as well as university application.
