description: The International Students STEM Organization was founded with the desire to create a more unified STEM community for students locally while also creating a global network of students in STEM who will work together to solve problems that exist around the world. We organise STEM events and competitions with other schools around the world to further engage in STEM and create a better world.

name: Sports Psychology
description: The Sports Psychology Society has been set up by students who have a strong understanding and interest in sports and psychology. We are keen to expand the understanding about psychology within sports for ourselves and others, providing members with a deeper and more diversified understanding regarding psychological aspects in sports. We are inviting students with interests in such topics to actively participate in discussions and activities related to Sports Psychology.

Additionally, the club may strive to promote teamwork, discipline, and a sense of community through collaborative musical endeavors. Overall, the Chinese Percussion Ensemble seeks to enrich the cultural experiences of its members while preserving and celebrating the heritage of Chinese percussion.

description: Judo is first and foremost intended to be a system of physical and mental development for the benefit of the body. It has a positive effect on strengthening the body and increasing its physical efficiency. Judo teaches discipline and tolerance, and it is an excellent sport for students to stay fit and healthy.Mr Stephen Chee has been coaching at UWCSEA for several years and has a positive rapport with the students. He is currently the President of Ju-Jitsu Kobudo Singapore, the President of Aiki-Jutso Takeda-Rya Maroto-ha Singapore, the Deputy Instructor of Singapore Shotokan Karate Do Club, the Master of Krav Maga, and the Guro Arnis and Kali. name: Singapore Book Club Exploring Singapore through Literature (New Members registration only!)
description: Why was the Singapore Book Club created?Ms Reid explains: 'I went to an international school in Hong Kong from the age of 12 to 18, and in that time, I didn't learn much about Hong Kong in class. I was lucky that all Hong Kong international schools have at least 30% local students. I was also lucky that I had an amazing English teacher while I was 16-18 who opened my eyes by giving me books and recommendations of books about Hong Kong (non-fiction) and set in Hong Kong (fiction), which meant I graduated school with a true sense of what it means to be part of Hong Kong. With this in mind, I want to give UWC students a time and place where they can be curious about Singapore and learn more about the region, so they can know Singapore better.'What to expect:Ms Reid: 'I have a wide range of books that vary greatly in length, genre (including non-fiction), themes and language level - there really is something for everyone! This isn't some "exclusive thing"; everyone is welcome! You can browse and choose something independently, or chat with members or me to get suitable recommendations. We can also buy books you're seen or read to add to the library! During the activity time we read independently and pause to share what we've found interesting or learnt. It's very casual, you can change to a new book whenever you wish. I will always check a book out to you for home, but if you don't read between sessions that's ok. All I'm looking for is that you come along each week ready to discover something new about Singapore.'What the Singapore Book Club isn't:Unlike traditional book clubs, we do not all read the same book at the same time (although if you and a friend would like to, that's ok too!) You also don't need to read the book you choose outside of the activity time, although you might like to and are welcome to do so!

name: Karate (Brown & Higher)
description: This is a non violent form of Karate and the training is provided by the Supreme Karate Do Association. The main contents include calisthenics, blocking, attacking, and evading attack, meditation, internal breathing exercises and power focusing. Mr Ang conducts his classes in both English and Mandarin to benefit all those children who speak either or both languages. It is proving to be very popular. Mr Ang has 36 years experience and holds a 4th Dan black belt. He has been coaching at UWCSEA for over 30 years and is extremely dedicated. He is particularly good with those who need extra guidance.A uniform will cost between $80 $170 depending on size. Grading takes place once every two Seasons and varies from $55 - $275 (depending on the number of tests). name: WATER POLO (PX Team)
description: Welcome to our Water Polo PX team where the love for team sports meets the excitement of aquatic play!Whether you're diving into water polo for the first time or aiming to enhance your skills, this program is designed for all levels of experience.We're passionate about matches and tournaments, adding that competitive edge to our fun-filled sessions.As part of our team, you'll get the chance to participate in thrilling matches and exciting tournaments.To ensure everyone's progress and teamwork, commitment is key.All players are asked to join us for training at least twice a week, honing skills and building strong camaraderie.If you're a confident swimmer, comfortable with breaststroke, freestyle, and backstroke, and can swim 100 meters without stopping in deep water, you're ready to join us.

name: 3D Printing Club (G6 - 12)
description: IDEAS Hub is delighted to host the 3D Printing Club this year!Join the UWCSEA 3D Printing Club to embark on an exciting journey into the world of technology, innovation, and creativity!Our club is dedicated to exploring the limitless possibilities of 3D printing technology while making a positive impact on the community. In our club, you will have the opportunity to: Learn hands-on 3D printing skills and techniques. Design and create your own 3D-printed projects. Collaborate with fellow students on innovative ventures. Engage in projects that contribute to sustainability and community service efforts. Showcase your creations through exhibitions and competitions. Develop essential skills such as problem-solving, communication, and teamwork.Have fun while unleashing your imagination and pushing the boundaries of innovation! Whether you're a beginner curious about 3D printing or an experienced enthusiast, everyone is welcome to join us on this exciting journey of exploration and creation.Come be a part of our vibrant community and let's make a difference together through the power of 3D printing! Sign up now and unleash your creativity with the UWCSEA 3D Printing Club!

name: Architecture Society
description: Architecture Society is a design focused activity which aims to cultivate positive and meaningful understandings about architecture and sculptural installation. With a focus on hands-on learning, participants may expect to learn basic design principles pertaining to building, drawing and sustainable modelling from an architectural perspective. Further, students will learn the significance of influential figures in architecture and how pivotal movements may shape their own works. Ultimately, this society aims to be a relaxed environment in which students are able to express their own interests and cultural identities with other like-minded individuals.

name: Mathematics Society
description: The Mathematics Society helps students explore different aspects of Mathematics. In the first two terms, there will be more focus on strengthening Mathematics skills with high-level problems selected from IGCSE and IB as well as math competitions such as UKMT, AMC10/12, SMKC, SASMO, and etc. Throughout the year, students will be highly encouraged to participate in these mathematics competitions. In the last term, students will be able to participate in the TANGENT magazine production by conducting investigations and mathematical analysis on their selected topic of interest. Students will have the option to work in pairs or alone to write practice IAs with assistance from IB students and teachers to be featured in the TANGENT magazine. Through participating in the Mathematics Society, students will be able to develop proficiency and delve into the study of mathematics.

name: The Phoenix
description: The Phoenix is UWCSEA Dover High School's new and official newspaper, carrying the headlines and voices that matter to students. With several different departments ranging from Don't Be Afraid which reports on college affairs to Impressions that focuses on Art, and even a dedicated business and finance section for budding investors, the Phoenix offers an unparalleled opportunity for students to exercise their right to expression, allowing them to share their views with the wider student body on a smorgasbord of mundane and complex issues.Business and Finance - Monday, 12:45pm - 1:15pmPoliticsXLaw - Tuesday 12:45pm - 1:15pmCollege Affairs - Thursday, 12:45pm - 1:15pm ; Friday, 1:15pm - 1:45pmImpressions - no regular meetingPhoenix Radio - no regular meeting (yet)

description: EnviroX is an innovative initiative that brings together the best of both worlds humanities and STEM to tackle pressing environmental issues. Through podcasts, articles, community events, and research projects, we explore the complex relationship between humans and the environment. Our mission is to inspire and equip the next generation of environmental leaders with the knowledge, creativity, and empathy needed to create a sustainable future.

By joining Bytes of Tech, you will have the opportunity to grow many skills such as communication, writing skills, be more knowledgable about the tech industry and connect with many fascinating people.

After this activity, students will understand art concepts clearer and be able to sketch more professionally than before. The sessions will be planned based on each student s ability, so that everyone feels comfortable, and is able to make progress.

name: Tea Talks
description: Tea Talks: A Safe Space for Open ConversationsTea Talks is a supportive group that meets weekly to discuss what s on your mind. Whether you're navigating serious topics like gender identity, seeking advice on building lasting relationships, or managing school life, this is a safe and inclusive space for meaningful conversations.What We Offer:In-Depth Discussions: Students will engage in thoughtful conversations on real-world issues, such as gender identity, feminism, and global menstrual challenges. These discussions aim to foster understanding, empathy, and the sharing of diverse perspectives.Biweekly Major Topics: Every two weeks, we dive into a broad and engaging topic to spark deeper reflection and dialogue among participants.Weekly Focus Areas: Weekly sessions cover practical and relatable issues like balancing academic responsibilities with personal relationships or planning for the future.Why Join?Tea Talks provides a welcoming and supportive environment where students can openly share their concerns, listen to others' experiences, and collaboratively explore solutions. By participating, students build a sense of community while developing a deeper understanding of the world around them and their own journey.Join us for a cup of tea and conversations that matter!

name: Game Theory Society
description: Game Theory is essentially about the strategic interactions between players in a competitive environment. It is widely used as a tool to analyse situations within a variety of fields such as economics, political science, and sociology. The aim of this club is to develop an interest in game theory and its applications by studying events such as the trade war, rivalry between firms, and political campaigns, from a rational decision-making perspective.

Alternatively, please send your vocal audition recordings to mfo@uwcsea.edu.sg by the end of week 4 (They can be audio or video and should be one and a half to three minutes long).1)UWC Day:Friday, 19th September, lunchtime performance under the tent.2)TOCCATA 2025:Saturday, 22nd November, 5.30pm, Main Hall. Dress rehearsals earlier in the day3)Opus 2026:Tuesday, 3rd March, 7:30 pm, Esplanade Concert Hall. Dress rehearsals Saturday 28th February and Sunday 1st March, Music Center.

As of August 2021, Prism will follow a hybrid sign-up format: It will open as part of the school s activity sign up for students who wish to have Prism on their official activities list. This may be appealing for IB students who would like to use it for CAS, or any other student who would like an official record of their attendance. It may also be appealing for all students (from Grade 9 to Grade 12) who would like to take on ambassador roles such as communicating with other student and staff bodies,etc. However, Prism will continue to operate as a drop-in group for any students who would prefer not to have Prism included in their activity report sent home to parents or any school attendance reports. Members must demonstrate excellent musicianship and discipline to perform challenging professional repertoire, providing a rewarding experience.Please use thisFormif you would like to be considered for one of our department string orchestras - New to the school, not currently playing in one of our orchestras or would like to be considered for a different group than your current allocation.For more information please contact Ms Tessa Augustyniak tma@uwcsea.edu.sg.

name: Intermediate Jazz Band
description: The Intermediate Jazz Band comprises saxophones, trumpets and trombones along with the rhythm section of drums, piano, bass and guitar. The band works on repertoire including swing, funk and latin jazz. The focus in rehearsals will be on sectional work, where each section of the band will play and work together. Players will develop ensemble awareness and section balance, style, phrasing, articulation and technique. We will be working toward a recording session of the pieces. Please note that this is an auditioned band and in the first instance you will need to contact Miles Tranter at mtr@gapps.uwcsea.edu.sg to arrange an audition time. Once auditioned and successful then you will be signed up by Mr Tranter.

name: Chinese History and Culture Club(For Chinese native speakers) New Applicant Sign up
description: China is one of the world s four ancient civilizations which has a rich history and culture. Learning Chinese history and culture opens up a unique window into understanding Chinese government, economics, inventions, philosophy, language and different ethnic groups. In order to introduce these knowledges to students, to help them to build the appreciation of China s past and present, as well as their cultural identities, a Chinese History & culture afterschool activity will be opened to G5-G12 students who are non-native Chinese speakers in Dover Campus. This will provide a great opportunity for students to be immersed in Chinese history and culture, whilst broadening their horizon and improving their Chinese language.

name: BioChem Society

From discussions about breakthroughs in biotechnology to explaining the concept of cell metabolism, we will cover a broad spectrum of scientific topics relevant to the modern world. No matter if you are an aspiring scientist or just want to consolidate your learning in science class, everyone will have something to enjoy!

name: Model Philosophy Ethics Olympiad (MPEO)
description: The Ethics Olympiad, an event characterised by competition and cooperation, enables students to engage in discussions on current, real-life ethical dilemmas. This unique initiative was first brought to life by Matthew Wills in 2013, inspired by similar events like the Ethics Bowl in the United States. The Olympiad differentiates itself from traditional debates by allowing students to advocate for beliefs they genuinely uphold, rather than assign opposing positions. They are recognized for demonstrating a deeper, more thoughtful, and logical understanding of the cases at hand.The core values of this activity are creativity, critical thinking, respect, collaboration and communication. The activity encourages students to deliberate on moral issues together, mirroring the complex moral and political interactions of our society.Students participating in this activity will be taught in a way that prepares them for attending the competition. The primary objectives of the activity are promoting ethical consciousness, critical thinking, respectful dialogue, international collaboration, and an appreciation for a diversity of views. At the end of the day, students will be able to understand more about philosophical theories and how ethics plays a role in the real world, in the meantime also applying them to debates to pop each other's mind.

name: Food Science Society
description: Food Science SocietyWe are a community for high school students passionate about exploring the science and technology behind the food we eat. Our goal is to help students understand the processes involved in how food is made, preserved, and processed, while also delving into the biology, chemistry, and nutritional aspects that shape the meals we consume every day.This society is perfect for students interested in pursuing fields such as food science, biology, chemistry, or nutrition, as well as for those who simply have a love for food and curiosity about its scientific background. Through discussions, activities, and explorations, we aim to provide a deeper understanding of the integration of science and technology into our daily lives.

name: Chinese Magazine Society
description: The Chinese Magazine Society (CMS) provides a distinctive learning environment where students interested in Chinese language and culture may connect with others through the creation and publishing of magazines. The society fosters communication, exchange of ideas, and improvement of cultural comprehension among its members. Students who participate in CMS learn plenty about contemporary Chinese culture and writing related skill sets. Overall, CMS is an exciting place for cultural diversification and personal development.

name: History Society
description: Sign up for History Soc! History Society is an inclusive and thriving community of history lovers. Every week, we spark debates on controversial historical events, encourage our members to share their historical artefacts, or play history-related games like History Bowl. Our discussion topics cover a range of periods and regions, including Tokugawa Japan, the Sri Lankan Civil War and the atomic bomb. After each session, our members walk away with renewed passion and understanding of history.This year, we are also collaborating with the English Department and Magical Murals service to create a visual History Timeline display along the corridors of the English block. We hope this project will bolster interdisciplinary learning by helping students place literary texts within historical contexts and connect concepts across space and time.No need to take a history course to join History Soc! If you love history or simply want to learn how our world has become what it s like today, then do not hesitate to sign up. We hope to see you at ART207 on Friday lunchtime!

name: HER Journey (G9 & 10) (Sep start)
description: Students must be prepared to attend occasional events with MDWs on Sundays.HER Journey is a Migrant Domestic Worker (MDW) skills training and advocacy group. Past workshops have focussed on: Canva website building; Capcut video production; MS Word; MS Excel; Gmail; and public speaking. In the coming year, HER Journey s main focus will continue to be on practical skills lessons that improve the participants employability and self-confidence.HER Journey is looking for students who truly care about MDWs and are willing to devote both time and skills to supporting them in appropriate ways. This is an opportunity to develop coach-mentorship and interpersonal skills. In particular, we are looking for students who are imaginative and collaborative, as we are constantly thinking of ways we can better support MDWs. Still, we are looking for genuine commitment and willingness to learn rather than relevant experience.For additional information, please go tohttps://herjourney.sg/about-us/

name: SIA MINDS (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a selective position for 2 Grade 11 students who will coach and support Middle School students doing the service. Interested students will be interviewed by Ms Sally Newman (MS Service Coordinator). To be considered, you will ideally have experience of doing service with people with intellectual disabilities. You will need to show evidence of the skills and qualities to mentor others. A Grade 11 coach-mentor must be both a role model for the younger students and someone who observes them and provides positive feedback. The coach-mentor must also accept guidance from the teacher/s leading the service.The group will be responsible for building a relationship of trust with their MINDS friends. They will also need to take responsibility for planning appropriate activities from week to week. The Grade 11 coach-mentor should coordinate a reflection and planning session at the end of each week s action . In addition, they should lead a more formal SWOT reflection around November and March, ideally including representatives from MINDS.In summary, this is a role for a committed, mature and sensitive individual who will aim to make the weekly interactions as positive as possible for our MINDS friends.The SIA MINDS centre aims to provide over 300 intellectually disabled young adults with the social and vocational skills they need for integration into society. One of the practical skills that clients learn is the packaging of audio headsets for Singapore Airlines (SIA), alongside baking and painting. During their afternoon break, a few of the clients move to the gym where they do activities organised by UWCSEA students. This includes ball games, dancing games and relay races - a chance for the clients to have some relaxation and fun.Note: In the eventuality of traffic problems students may occasionally not be able to get 4.30pm bus.For additional information, please go tohttps://www.minds.org.sg/for-adults/supported-employment-programme/smedc/

name: EmpowerAge Service (Sunlove Active Ageing Centre)
description: Work with vibrant seniors in fun, interactive sessions that include strength and conditioning, balance games, reaction-time challenges, and brain gym activities. Guided by an experienced PE teacher, you ll design and lead programs that keep minds and bodies moving perfect for students interested in Sports Science or careers in physio/occupational therapy. Build skills, share laughs, and make a real impact in an inspiring community.

name: Aqua-fun Fitness for Seniors
description: Note that this is alate afternoonservice on Friday (4:30-5:30pm) - an uplifting way to start the weekend! It does not require advanced swimming skills because the focus is onfitnessactivities in water up to 1.2 metres. A deeper objective of the service is to provide the visiting seniors with a positive social experience, build their water confidence and help them grow stronger. Some sessions are likely to end with a gentle game of water polo in the shallow pool.

name: HOME Activities Service
description: Assorted activities with migrant domestic workers from the HOME (Humanitarian Organisation for Migrant Economics) shelter. Students will prepare activities in response to the women's interests/requests. Examples might include English language skills, computer skills, baking/cooking, sports, games (with small prizes), music, dance, crafts, poster design, and phone photography. Where students lack the necessary skills, they may need to identify suitably skilled students/staff outside the group to train them. It is important to keep in mind that a positive social experience and confidence building are just as important to the HOME women as skills development.

name: Here2Hear (trained mentors)
description: (These roles are only open to students who were trained in peer counselling during Grade 10)Here2Hear is a year-long student service group dedicated to supporting mental health and promoting wellness through meaningful peer connections. As a member, you ll help raise awareness, support fellow students one-on-one, and lead campus initiatives that foster a more caring and connected school community. This is a great opportunity to develop your communication skills and emotional intelligence while making a real difference. We re looking for thoughtful, empathetic students who want to lead through listening and help create a school where every voice is heard. We re Here2Hear.In addition to planning and delivering activities that promote wellness and wellbeing around the campus, you will also be responsible for spending time outside of Friday lunches developing activities and resources for a peer counselling training course.

name: eSecond Life (Sep start)
description: Students will learn to erase data from used computers, do necessary repairs and install basic programs so that the computers can be reused. In addition, you will possibly do basic repairs on used smartphones. The group will also arrange for distribution of equipment to suitable Service partners.

name: Early Morning Gardening (Sep start)
description: Early morning gardening service - raising plants from seeds and cuttings, planting and maintaining them in beds around the campus, collaborating with our landscaping department on areas to target. You will get your morning mindfulness in the campus gardens by growing plants that encourage butterflies and pollinators and watering our edible crops. You will collect dried plants for teas and cordial and floral displays. You will harvest herbs and produce for community give-away harvest festivals.

name: Early Morning Gardening (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: Early morning gardening service - raising plants from seeds and cuttings, planting and maintaining them in beds around the campus, collaborating with our landscaping department on areas to target. name: Campus Biodiversity (Sep start)
description: Campus Biodiversity is a hands-on environmental service project dedicated to protecting and improving the school s natural habitats. Our work includes surveying and recording campus wildlife, maintaining dragonfly ponds through weekly cleaning and water quality testing, monitoring ecological changes, planting native species and a lot more. We also support bird conservation by setting up artificial nesting boxes for species like hornbills and owls, while engaging the school community in hands-on activities to promote biodiversity awareness.

name: Incredible Edible - Herb Garden (Thurs) (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: This is a position for a Grade 11 student who will coach younger (G9/10) students. Interested students will be interviewed. To be considered, you will need to show evidence of a personal commitment to sustainable development through previous action or service learning. The selected students will be expected to support the teachers who lead our weekly Grade 9-10 Incredible Edible groups. The core activities are very practical - propagating edible herbs in the nursery, sowing seeds and planting cuttings. You will also remove weeds from planting spaces, prepare the soil, plant out seedlings, and do mulching and watering as necessary. The Grade 11 coach-mentors must be a "hands on" role model but should also act as a mentor and educator, helping students see the connection between their practical chores and the larger objective of a sustainable future. Their role will include running occasional Reflection meetings.

You will harvest herbs and produce for community give-away harvest festivals.

name: Pedalkart (Engineers & Designers)
description: IDEAS Hub is excited to launch Pedalkart Activity (Designers & Engineers) for Season 1-4!Pedalkart provides students with a unique chance to dive into the world of design and engineering while working collaboratively in teams. Through hands-on experience, students will improve their problem-solving skills, creativity, and technical abilities as they work together to build and design their own pedal-powered vehicles.Peer-to-peer mentoring will play a key role in refining teamwork, leadership, and technical skills in a dynamic and supportive environment.As the activity progresses, students will also have the opportunity to participate in the PedalKart Challenge in June 2026.Looking ahead, there will be future leadership roles available in areas such as:Team managementEngineering and designEvent coordinationTechnical support and innovation

name: Esports - HS Street Fighter 6 (G9 - 12)
description: IDEAS Hub is thrilled to host the Esports Street Fighter 6 this season!Esports offers students a fantastic opportunity to enhance their skills in a collaborative team setting. Through peer-to-peer mentoring and coaching, team members will develop their mental stacking abilities, fine-motor skills, and game expertise, all while having fun in a supportive and inclusive environment.There will be additional chances to participate in inter-school and local competitions (optional).As the activity grows and develops, there will be future leadership opportunites for involvement in some of the following elements of esports:- Team management- Coaching- Business and sponsorship- Technology support and management

name: Plastic Fantastic (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: The selected Grade 11 student will be expected to prepare weekly sessions in collaboration with the service leader, manage and motivate Middle School students, lead structured group reflections, and ensure that the MS students not only acquire relevant practical skills but also an understanding of how the service activity fits into our sustainability objectives. The coach-mentor must prioritise safe working practices. They must be an effective and reflective communicator.

name: Chess for Beginners (MS/HS)
description: Our Beginner programme is geared to aspiring players with little to no prior experience with chess and are eager to learn the basics of the game. It is designed not just to teach students the rules of chess, but to instil a deep love for the game. Additionally, it serves as a vital stepping stone for students to progress to higher levels offered by UWC, such as the Intermediate Group and the School Team.This course is designed to run on a seasonal basis and is intended to be taken once. After completing it, you are free to advance to the intermediate group.

name: Volleyball U19 Competitive (Wednesday Evening)
description: This competitive volleyball program is designed to help athletes reach their full potential by incorporating advanced biomechanical analysis and performance testing. These tools offer athletes valuable, data-driven insights to fine-tune their techniques, improve movement efficiency, and minimize injury risk.At the heart of the program is high-intensity, team-based performance training. Participants will take part in open-ended, game-like drills that mirror real match scenarios, enhancing their tactical thinking, court awareness, and responsiveness under pressure.The program also focuses on developing sustained physical fitness and mental resilience. By training in a challenging and supportive environment, athletes will strengthen their focus, build endurance, and cultivate the competitive mindset needed to perform at a high level.

name: Touch U16 & U20 (Skills Development - Small Group)
description: The programme targets the developing core ball skills and focuses on performing skills in modified Touch drills. Students can enjoy sessions through station work, skill execution, correction and progressions. These activities shift towards building a strong foundation skill set and application of techniques in game situations.

name: Infant. Classrooom Support (Fri / Emily Nocita/K2ENO)
description: If you are doing an Infant School or Junior School classroom support service during High School lunchtime, you must not waste time going to the canteen. You are expected to be in the classroom actively helping by 12:55pm. Please take a packed lunch to school on your Service day and agree with the classroom teacher on a suitable spot where you can eat before entering the class. (An alternative is to eat at 1:35.)You will work closely with the classroom teacher to help K1, K2 or Grade 1 children with learning tasks. This might include general help or a more specific focus on a few children, for example reading to them or doing number work. You muenjoy interacting with young children, have good communication skills and be able to use your initiative. Commitment is for the whole academic year and full attendance is required.(Fri: Phonics - working with learners who need extra support.)

name: Home Languages Programme Coach-Mentor (Chinese C3 / Tuesdays / 3.10pm - 4.10pm)
description: Home Languages Programme Coach-MentorThis is a unique Grade 11 service for a small number of students. It is only suitable for students who arefluent users of the relevant languageand can also demonstrate that they haveappropriate skillsfor supporting younger learners, as well the creativity and commitment to promote the home language beyond the classroom. Interested students will be interviewed by Home Languages Programme (HLP) staff. We can consider only speakers ofBahasa Indonesia, Chinese, Dutch, French, German, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Russian, Spanish and Swedish.The core weekly tasks will include welcoming (possibly accompanying) children to their classroom, interacting with them in the relevant language, sometimes supporting the HLP teacher with the delivery of lesson content, supporting individual learners with tasks and occasionally assisting with tasks such as photocopying. In addition, all leaders must investigate and put into practice opportunities to publicize and celebrate their language beyond the classroom. This will include events at the UN International Mother Tongue week (in February) but could include displays and occasional pop-up events. Planning these will require collaboration with language teachers, support staff, fellow students and groups concerned with promoting diversity, equity and inclusion at UWCSEA.In summary, this is not for someone who simply speaks the right language and likes the idea of an easy on-campus service. We need passionate commitment, creativity, good interpersonal communication and a general desire to boost the status of home languages.

name: Robotics Club (G9 - 12)
description: IDEAS Hub is thrilled to welcome students to our Robotics Club this year! This club is perfect for anyone curious about electronics, coding, or engineering skills no matter your skill level.Participants will have the chance to learn fundamental engineering skills while exploring exciting hands-on projects. Students can join our MateROV competitive teams and challenge other schools in Singapore in an underwater robotics tournament. Along the way, we ll build knowledge and skills with platforms like Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and more. Members are also encouraged to develop their own personal or recreational projects.In addition to MateROV, students will have opportunities to explore:VEX RoboticsFIRST Robotics Competition (FRC)Hyundai Robotics Challenge (in collaboration with Hyundai Motor Group)Whether you re here to compete, create, or just tinker, there s a place for you in Robotics Club!

name: Magical Murals (G11 Coach-Mentor)
description: The Grade 11 coach-mentor will be responsible for assisting the staff leader to plan and manage the practical work of a Grade 9-10 group painting murals in various spaces around the Dover campus. Many of the murals represent our UWCSEA mission and values. The coach-mentor is also expected to investigate opportunities for the group to design and paint murals for some of our Service partners and/or communities in the Dover Road neighbourhood. In the past, this group has done murals at Red Cross Home and Institute of Mental Health. Closer neighbours, such as St John's St Margaret's (SJSM) Nursing Home and NUH, may also want to welcome the group. The Grade 11 student may also arrange mural events at more distant locations - probably during weekends - involving students/staff from outside the weekly service. (In such cases, it would be essential to arrange parental permission and have a UWCSEA staff member present for safeguarding reasons.)The coach-mentor will be expected to guide the younger students, emphasising safety, responsible handling of materials and collaborative effort.

name: Sports Analysis
description: In this activity, students will watch and analyse sports videos and live events to study how different injuries occur in athletes. They will explore the short- and long-term effects of these injuries on athletic performance and careers, while also considering the role of sports medicine in treatment and prevention. Through group discussion and case studies, students will develop critical thinking, teamwork, and empathy as they connect real-life sports moments with health and wellbeing.

name: Home Languages Programme Coach-Mentor (Japanese C5 / Wednesday / 3.10pm - 4.10pm)
description: Home Languages Programme Coach-MentorThis is a unique Grade 11 service for a small number of students. It is only suitable for students who arefluent users of the relevant languageand can also demonstrate that they haveappropriate skillsfor supporting younger learners, as well the creativity and commitment to promote the home language beyond the classroom. Interested students will be interviewed by Home Languages Programme (HLP) staff. We can consider only speakers ofBahasa Indonesia, Chinese, Dutch, French, German, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Russian, Spanish and Swedish.The core weekly tasks will include welcoming (possibly accompanying) children to their classroom, interacting with them in the relevant language, sometimes supporting the HLP teacher with the delivery of lesson content, supporting individual learners with tasks and occasionally assisting with tasks such as photocopying. In addition, all leaders must investigate and put into practice opportunities to publicize and celebrate their language beyond the classroom. This will include events at the UN International Mother Tongue week (in February) but could include displays and occasional pop-up events. Planning these will require collaboration with language teachers, support staff, fellow students and groups concerned with promoting diversity, equity and inclusion at UWCSEA.In summary, this is not for someone who simply speaks the right language and likes the idea of an easy on-campus service. We need passionate commitment, creativity, good interpersonal communication and a general desire to boost the status of home languages.

name: Peacebuilders Coach-Mentor
description: Peacebuilders is a structured series of workshops for Grade 9-10 students on how to promote peace in our personal relationships and in the communities in which we belong. Among the themes will be equity, inclusion and justice. The Grade 12 coach-mentors will have been trained in the Initiative for Peace (IFP) programme and will lead the workshops under the guidance of two teaching staff.

name: Korean Drumming
description: Learning, practising and performing Traditional Korean Drumming. This has been a successful activity in the College for over 15 years

name: Fashion Design : Portfolio Building
description: Explore the full process of becoming a fashion designer.Students begin with brainstorming and moodboards, building a clear theme, color story, and visual language.They conduct primary and secondary research to ground concepts in real references.Through textile/sample making, students experiment with materials, textures, and surface techniques. In sketch illustration, ideas become silhouettes and detailed garment drawings.Finally, students learn portfolio layout curating pages that show process, iteration, and outcomes and present work in a supportive review session. By the end, each student leaves with polished portfolio pages, stronger visual communication skills, and a deeper understanding of how designers think, test, and refine.

name: Golf - Elite Junior Program
description: This weekly training program is designed to help junior golfers build strong technical foundations, develop consistency, and cultivate a love for the game in a supportive environment.Conducted at the driving range to improve ball striking and swing mechanics, each 90-minute session combines structured skill development, supervised practice, and fun challenges with bay tracers to keep young golfers motivated andengaged.Our coaching approach is built on the principle of developing complete, competition-ready golfers.Each session is designed to provide a comprehensive, high-performance training experience that refines every element of the game, ensuring players are prepared both technically and mentally for success.We also recognise that each player arrives with different levels of experience and ability. Our program is carefully tailored to meet the needs of every individual, ensuring that every golfer - whether developing fundamentals or refining advanced techniques - receives the optimal level of challenge and support.

name: Dance Society
description: Dance Society combines theory and practice to explore dance as a creative and cultural expression. Weekly sessions include discussions, games, and practical workshops in choreography, improvisation, and performance, while monthly projects focus on creating and teaching choreography. Open to all levels, the society offers a space to build confidence, creativity, and collaboration through movement.

name: Puzzle Society
description: We learn critical thinking skills by understanding how puzzles are made and how their solved. Every other lesson we design and create a puzzle to be added to the final result being an escape room to share with our community.

name: PPSEAWA Youth Public Health Club
description: The PPSEAWA Youth Public Health Club is a student-led community for those passionate about health, leadership, and making a positive impact.We explore topics such as disease prevention, mental well-being, women s health, and community care.Members collaborate with PPSEAWA Singapore, organize workshops, lead service projects, and develop essential skills in leadership, teamwork, and communication, while contributing to the well-being of our local and regional communities.

name: F1 In Schools UWCSEA
description: This activity will allow you to dive into the world of motorsports and experience how it feels to collaborate with a team in order to build a functioning miniature F1 car. Our 30 person team will be split into five groups: social media/marketing team, fundraising team, car design team, manufacturing team, and display team. The top 6 most ambitious participants in the team will be selected to represent our school in the F1 in schools regional and global competition. Participating in this team will allow you to kickstart your dreams of working in motorsports!

name: Neuroscience Society
description: The Neuroscience Club is an engaging and fun space for students to explore both foundational and in-depth neuroscience. Members will learn key terminology, brain anatomy, neuron structure and classification, glial cells, neural communication, memory, cognition, and many other topics. The club introduces students to fundamental concepts while also progressing toward advanced, college-level neuroscience. Hands-on inquiry will be encouraged through case study research, dissections or cognitive mini-games, and student presentations. Members will also discuss ethical and societal issues, fostering collaboration, problem-solving, and critical thinking. For students already interested in neuroscience, the club provides exposure to advanced ideas, future careers, and academic pathways. For those without prior experience, it offers an interactive and enjoyable introduction that may spark new curiosity. By the end, students will be able to explain neuroscience concepts, connect biology and psychology, and apply their learning through teamwork and research.

name: Music Charity Crescendo
description: This program is a student-led music activity that provides a platform for students to explore their passion for music and develop their skills as performers, songwriters, and collaborators. Throughout the year, members will plan and host seasonal concerts together, taking on various roles, including backstage crews. All the profits we make from the concerts will be donated to the Singapore Children's Society. We can use our passion for music to make a meaningful difference in both ourselves and our community.

name: Morning Org Group
description: This activity is for students that find executive functioning and daily organization challenging. Topics that will be covered include; weekly planning and organization, how to create useful task lists, how to stay focused in class, how to plan responses in assessments, how to engage and have positive intent. This is a strengths based program and we would look at how to do these things in a positive way.

name: Quantum Society
description: This activity provides a deeper insight into the novel field of quantum computing. We will be working towards the ThiQuHACK (interdisciplinary Quantum HACKathon), MIT's annual quantum hackathon. Students interested in programming, quantum mechanics, physics, and advanced mathematics are encouraged to join. This activity will begin from a beginner level's understanding of quantum computing and aid students in their journey of understanding quantum computing.

name: Fashionomics Society
description: The Fashionomics Society connects fashion and economics by applying key economic concepts to real-world issues in the fashion industry. Members will explore topics such as sustainability, consumer behaviour, labour practices, and global market dynamics through discussions, case studies, and projects. By bridging creativity and theory, the club encourages critical thinking and global awareness, helping students understand how individual choices shape a more sustainable and equitable future.

name: Campus Gardening (Feb start)
description: This is one of our most important environmental initiatives on campus: without compost, we would have no Incredible Edible, Campus Gardening, Urban Rooftop Gardening or Rainforest Restoration. Most Singapore soil is not suitable for successful gardening or tree planting. It needs to be enriched with compost, which is basically "plant food" in the form of decomposed vegetables, fruits, leaves etc.In "Feed the Earth" service, you will learn about the science of composting and help with the practical chores that turn food waste into rich black soil for the College gardens and endangered tree species. You will also be responsible for transporting compost by wheelbarrow to trees and gardens all over the campus and spreading this "mulch" around the plants. In addition, you will have a chance to do some gardening. All of our gardening and tree nursery services should really learn about and do composting because it is such an essential part of growing plants. Thank you for joining this special service and feeding the earth.

description: Most Singapore soil is not suitable for successful gardening or tree planting. It needs to be enriched with compost, which is basically plant food in the form of decomposed vegetables, fruits, leaves etc. Join our "compost factory" and help with the practical chores that turn mucky waste into rich black soil for the College gardens and endangered tree species. You will also be responsible for transporting compost by wheelbarrow to trees and gardens all over the campus and spreading this "mulch" around the plants. "Essentially, all life depends upon the soil There can be no life without soil and no soil without life; they have evolved together. (Dr. Charles E Kellogg, Soil Scientist and Chief of the USDA s Bureau for Chemistry and Soils)

name: Sports In Numbers
description: The Sports in Numbers Society is a student-led initiative that explores the intersection of sport, finance, and data. Open to all students with an interest in sport, business, or current events, the society meets weekly to discuss and analyse real-world topics such as franchise leagues, sponsorship deals, player transfers, and the economics of sports organisations. Sessions will include interactive activities such as quizzes, case studies, and open discussions. The society aims to create an engaging space where students can apply classroom concepts to global systems, develop critical thinking and communication skills, and connect their passion for sport with a deeper understanding of the world.

description: If you are doing an Infant School or Junior School classroom support service during High School lunchtime, you must not waste time going to the canteen. You are expected to be in the classroom actively helping by 12:55pm. Please take a packed lunch to school on your Service day and agree with the classroom teacher on a suitable spot where you can eat before entering the class. (An alternative is to eat at 1:35.)You will work closely with the classroom teacher to help K1, K2 or Grade 1 children with learning tasks. This might include general help or a more specific focus on a few children, for example reading to them or doing number work. You must enjoy interacting with young children, have good communication skills and be able to use your initiative. Commitment is for the whole academic year and full attendance is required.Note:Writers workshop - working with small groups on specific tasks planned by the teacher.Phonics - working with learners who need extra support.(Note: Classroom Inquiry - you will support with the children as they explore the classroom.)

It also develops students' critical thinking skills through competition questions that require more than just biology knowledge.

name: Badminton Phoenix Squad (Competitive)
description: The Phoenix Elite Badminton Programme is a high-pressure, invitation-only space for athletes who are ready to compete at the top. This isn't just another practice; it is a total transformation of how you play and train. Every session is built to break down your old habits and rebuild your game from the ground up through intense drills that sharpen your skills, speed, power, and mental toughness. Getting in is a privilege, and staying in simply requires the commitment to giving your best on the court.

name: Chinese Cultural Story, Folklore, and Philosophy Club
description: This club will provide a unique space for students to connect with their heritage while developing critical thinking skills through the lens of traditional wisdom and it's core Content is:Literary Classics: Exploring idiom stories ( ), fables ( 1 ), and classical poetry/songs ( ).Folklore & Traditions: Introducing cultural icons and customs, such as the Mazu belief (maritime culture and compassion) and the history and social strategies behind Mahjong. Heritage

name: Design Venture Society
description: The Economy of Design Club explores how creative design and economic strategy intersect to shape products, services, and user experiences occupying a unique middle ground that is neither purely economics nor traditional design.This club focuses on the space where innovation, client psychology, aesthetics, and market value converge.Through case studies, hands-on challenges, and guest speakers, members investigate how companies apply design thinking to build stronger business and design models and more impactful brands, while also understanding the economic principles that explain why certain designs thrive.Each session integrates accessible theory with practical experimentation, covering topics such as user psychology, product identity, pricing strategy, and value creation.The Economy of Design Club is ideal for students curious about design and eager to understand the mechanisms and reasoning behind everyday innovations.

name: Poetry Society
description: Poetry Society is a creative space where writers come together to explore language, emotion, and self-expression through poetry.The society encourages experimentation, discussion, and sharing in a supportive community that values diverse voices and perspectives.It s a space where members grow both their confidence and craft.

name: The Marketing Society
description: The Marketing Society is a student-led initiative designed to explore the dynamic world of marketing and its influence on society, business, and culture.Through interactive discussions, case studies, and projects, members will learn about key marketing concepts such as branding, consumer behaviour, digital marketing, and campaign design.The society will also analyse real-world marketing strategies and advertisements to better understand how companies communicate and connect with audiences in different ways.The Marketing Society aims to create a safe space where students can explore their interests, develop transferable skills, and connect marketing theory with real-world applications.Our discussions includes: Why does beer brands like Heineken and Estrella Galicia Sponsor F1? How should brands handle cultural appropriation in advertising? AND MUCH MORE
